---
title: "Managing Software Environments"
toc: false
toc-location: body
---

Many genomics analyses rely on dozens of software packages, each with specific version requirements. Installing and updating these packages manually can lead to **version conflicts**, where one tool requires a different version of a dependency than another. This becomes especially problematic if you must run multiple analyses at once.

Environment management tools solve this problem by allowing you to create **isolated software environments** for each project. Each environment contains its own set of packages and versions, ensuring that different projects do not interfere with one another.

These tools also make analyses easier to **reproduce and share**. Environment details can be saved to files, allowing collaborators to recreate the exact software environment used in an analysis.

:::{.highlight-section}
Below are two commonly used tools for managing environments in genomics workflows.
:::

:::: {.resource-row}

:::{.resource-card data-href="/pages/resources/envs/conda.html"}
![](/images/icons/conda.png){.resource-icon}

Conda for Python
:::

:::{.resource-card data-href="/pages/resources/envs/renv.html"}
![](/images/icons/renv.svg){.resource-icon}

renv for R
:::

::::

<script>
document.querySelectorAll('.resource-card[data-href]').forEach(card => {
  card.addEventListener('click', () => {
    window.location.href = card.getAttribute('data-href');
  });
});
</script>
